note: jquery_ui_touch_punch is a pure asset-library provider (no config, permissions, plugins, or PHP API). It re-provides the Touch Punch shim as a Drupal asset library after jQuery UI was removed from core, so all cases are `recipe` (response-graded), not `execution` (live-state).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the jquery_ui_touch_punch module installed, what is the exact machine name of the asset library it declares that I would attach to add touch support? Give just the library id.
evals.expected_output: The module declares the asset library `jquery_ui_touch_punch/touch-punch`.

evals.prompt: In a Drupal 11 module I build a render array `$build` for a page that uses jQuery UI draggable. Show the one line that attaches Touch Punch so the widget also works on touch screens.
What jQuery UI asset library does the jquery_ui_touch_punch/touch-punch library depend on (so jQuery UI is loaded before the shim runs)? Name it.
evals.expected_output: It depends on the `jquery_ui/core` asset library.

evals.prompt: Before I can use jquery_ui_touch_punch on Drupal 11, which contrib module must also be installed as its dependency? Name the module.
evals.expected_output: It requires the jQuery UI module (`jquery_ui`), since jQuery UI was removed from core.

evals.prompt: Why would I install jquery_ui_touch_punch on a Drupal 11 site? Explain in one or two sentences what problem it solves for jQuery UI widgets.
evals.expected_output: jQuery UI mouse widgets (draggable, sortable, sliders, resizable) only respond to mouse events; Touch Punch is a shim that translates touchstart/touchmove/touchend into simulated mouse events so those widgets work on touch screens (phones/tablets).

evals.prompt: jquery_ui_touch_punch ships as a library provider but relies on an external JavaScript file. Where must that Touch Punch JS be placed on disk, and what is the underlying composer package that provides it?
evals.expected_output: The Touch Punch JS lives under `/libraries` (e.g. /libraries/jquery-ui-touch-punch/jquery.ui.touch-punch.min.js), provided by the composer package `politsin/jquery-ui-touch-punch`.

evals.prompt: Where is the settings/configuration page for jquery_ui_touch_punch on a Drupal 11 site, and what options does it expose?
evals.expected_output: It has no configuration, settings page, permissions, or UI; you simply attach the `jquery_ui_touch_punch/touch-punch` library where touch support is needed.
